2604.09075.title: Hierarchical Alignment: Enforcing Hierarchical Instruction-Following in LLMs through Logical Consistency
2604.09075.paper_summary: Large language models increasingly operate under multiple instructions from heterogeneous sources with different authority levels, including system policies, user requests, tool outputs, and retrieved context. While prior work on instruction hierarchy highlights the importance of respecting instruction priorities, it mainly focuses on adversarial attacks and overlooks the benign but common instruction conflicts that arise in real-world applications. In such settings, models must not only avoid security violations but also preserve task utility and behavioral consistency when instructions partially or implicitly conflict. We propose Neuro-Symbolic Hierarchical Alignment (NSHA) for hierarchical instruction-following by explicitly modeling and enforcing instruction priorities. At inference time, we introduce solver-guided reasoning that formulates instruction resolution as a constraint satisfaction problem, enabling the model to derive a maximally consistent set of applicable instructions under hierarchical constraints. At training time, NSHA distills solver-based decisions into model parameters using automatically constructed supervision. We evaluate our approach on rule following, task execution, tool use, and safety, covering both single-turn and multi-turn interactions, and show that NSHA significantly improves performance under such conflicts while maintaining competitive utility in reference settings.
2604.09075.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型越來越多地在來自不同權威層級的異質來源下運行多個指令，包括系統政策、用戶請求、工具輸出和檢索的上下文。<lb/>雖然先前的研究強調了遵循指令優先級的重要性，但主要集中於對抗性攻擊，忽略了在現實應用中出現的良性但常見的指令衝突。在這種情況下，模型不僅必須避免安全違規，還必須在指令部分或隱性衝突時保持任務效用和行為一致性。<lb/>我們提出了神經符號層次對齊（Neuro-Symbolic Hierarchical Alignment, NSHA），通過明確建模和強制執行指令優先級來實現層次指令跟隨。在推理時，我們引入了解決器引導推理，將指令解析形式化為約束滿足問題，使模型能夠在層次約束下推導出一組最大一致的適用指令。<lb/>在訓練時，NSHA 通過自動構建的監督將基於解決器的決策提煉為模型參數。我們在規則遵循、任務執行、工具使用和安全性方面評估我們的方法，涵蓋單回合和多回合互動，並顯示 NSHA 在這些衝突下顯著提高了性能，同時在參考設置中保持競爭效用。
2604.09075.author: Shu Yang et.al.
2604.09075.authors: Shu Yang, Zihao Zhou, Di Wang, Wenda Li
